heading: Interim preservation of certain rail passenger terminals
label: 5564 Interim preservation of certain rail passenger terminals

children.heading: .—General Grant Authority
children.label: a .—General Grant Authority
children.text: Subject to subsection (b) of this section, the Secretary of Transportation may make a grant of financial assistance to a responsible person (including a governmental authority) to preserve a rail passenger terminal under . To receive assistance under this section, the person must be qualified, prepared, committed, and authorized by law to maintain (and prevent the demolition, dismantling, or further deterioration of) the terminal until plans for its reuse are prepared.section 5562(a)(2) of this title

the Secretary decides the rail passenger terminal has a reasonable likelihood of being converted to, or conditioned for reuse as, an intermodal transportation terminal, a civic or cultural activities center, or both; and
planning activity directed toward conversion or reuse has begun and is proceeding in a competent way.

children.label: A
children.text: reasonably capable of conversion to intermodal transportation terminals;

children.label: B
children.text: listed in the National Register of Historic Places maintained by the Secretary of the Interior; or

children.label: C
children.text: recommended (on the basis of architectural integrity and quality) by the Advisory Council on Historic Preservation or the Chairman of the National Endowment for the Arts.
The Secretary of Transportation may not make a grant under this section for more than 80 percent of the total cost of maintaining the terminal for an interim period of not more than 5 years.
